# UC-15.1.10 · UPS Battery Runtime Remaining

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability

*We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.*

---

## Description

This use case tracks UPS battery autonomy from SNMP polling of UPS-MIB upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining, normalized into sourcetype=snmp:ups as runtime_min together with electrical load_pct, so operators see how many minutes the string can sustain the observed load.

## Value

When utility power drops, leadership needs a trustworthy minutes-to-empty signal to sequence generator checks, stagger virtual machine shutdown, and avoid unplanned hardware or data loss; watching runtime_min against load_pct turns a vague battery alarm into an actionable countdown for the right teams.

## Implementation

Poll upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining every 1–5 minutes via the configured SNMP TA path, persisting runtime_min and load_pct on sourcetype=snmp:ups; alert when runtime_min crosses the site policy (for example below ten minutes) or when the high-load branch in the saved search fires; correlate every page with concurrent generator transfer or maintenance windows recorded in facility change tools.

## Evidence

Export the saved UPS Battery Runtime Remaining alert definition plus a frozen Search query over index=power sourcetype=snmp:ups for the audited week, archived with timestamps that line up with facility incident tickets.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Lab UPS emits snmp:ups rows where runtime_min < 10 and load_pct > 70 for three polls and snmpwalk matches UPS-MIB upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining to Splunk runtime_min.

### Negative scenario

Core routing blocks UDP/161 between the Heavy Forwarder and UPS managers while SNMP TA inputs.conf stays enabled; index=power shows no fresh snmp:ups rows during the maintenance window but the UPS web console still reports healthy runtime_min, so correlation proves a collector-path outage rather than battery exhaustion deserving immediate load shed.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Deploy SNMP TA (UPS-MIB upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining) collection using Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app ID 6266) or SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app ID 1537) on a Heavy Forwarder that can reach UPS management interfaces over UDP/161 (or SC4SNMP-equivalent polling) and vault SNMPv3 credentials outside repository-tracked configs.
- Reserve index=power (or your contracted SNMP landing index) and confirm transforms or SC4SNMP processors emit sourcetype=snmp:ups with FIELDALIAS/EVAL mappings that populate runtime_min from upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining, load_pct, ups_name, location, and battery_status exactly as referenced by this UC.
- Grant operators a scoped Splunk RBAC role such as power_ops with search rights limited to index=power plus dependent lookups; separate scheduler identities should carry schedule_search without broad admin capabilities.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Author snmp modular-input or Splunk Connect for SNMP profiles that poll UPS-MIB upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining every one to five minutes (cron such as */5 * * * *), documenting OID-to-field parity in internal runbooks so firmware upgrades cannot silently rename counters.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the UC SPL into Search, constrain earliest=-15m@m during tuning, then save as an alert with dispatch windows aligned to the polling cadence; pipe results through table _time, ups_name, location, runtime_min, load_pct, battery_status for operator readability.

### Step 3 — Validate
Spot-check Splunk samples against an interactive snmpwalk of upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining for the same UPS management IP and timestamp skew; reconcile discrepancies before promoting thresholds.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish dashboards highlighting minimum runtime_min per site, route alert actions through email or ticketing, and capture Splunk audit trails showing who acknowledged each incident tied to snmp:ups feeds.

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups"
| where runtime_min < 10 OR (runtime_min < 20 AND load_pct > 70)
| table _time, ups_name, location, runtime_min, load_pct, battery_status
```

## Visualization

Single-value tile for minimum runtime_min across ups_name for the site; trellis or gauge row per UPS showing runtime_min with color thresholds; line or area chart of runtime_min over time with load_pct overlaid as a second axis; optional table drilldown sorted by ascending runtime_min with location and battery_status for war-room triage.

## Known False Positives

• Facility generator transfer exercises intentionally shed UPS load—runtime_min swings wildly even though utility feed returns seconds later; distinguish using CMMS work orders tagged generator_test and suppress alerts inside the published change window keyed by ups_name.
• SNMP Modular Input worker restarts on the Heavy Forwarder create synchronized poll gaps across every ups_name while onsite snmpwalk still returns healthy UPS-MIB counters; throttle paging until splunk_server heartbeat metrics confirm collectors resumed.
• Planned UPS firmware battery calibration raises load_pct spikes without true outage risk; correlate battery_status transitions logged on snmp:ups and demand two consecutive violating polls before notifying.
• Network ACL rollouts block UDP/161 briefly causing stale runtime_min values to linger in index=power; verify with traceroute or socket tests from the forwarder rather than declaring a depleted string.
